source.title: What we lose when we outsource our humanity to AI | Catholic Futurist

summary.title_hr: Što gubimo kada prepuštamo svoju ljudskost umjetnoj inteligenciji | Katolički futurist
summary.abstract_hr: Ova epizoda podcasta istražuje duboke izazove koje donosi umjetna inteligencija (AI) i njezin utjecaj na ljudskost. Gosti raspravljaju o opasnostima outsourcinga ljudskih funkcija, transhumanizmu i potencijalnoj 'egzistencijalnoj litici' zbog duhovnog siromaštva. Naglašava se ključna uloga Crkve u pružanju nade i vodstva, te se istražuju mogućnosti korištenja AI-ja za evangelizaciju i poticanje ljudskog procvata u digitalnom dobu.
key_points: Tehnologija, posebno AI, potiče outsourcing ljudskih funkcija i sposobnosti, što dovodi do epistemskog pomaka u načinu spoznavanja svijeta i udaljava nas od utjelovljenog i društvenog učenja.
Pretjerano oslanjanje na lakoću i učinkovitost tehnologije izbjegava stvarnost da je rad ugrađen u čovjeka i da se kroz borbu s realnošću razvija intelekt i duša.
Postoji zabrinutost da će ljudi, s više slobodnog vremena zbog AI-ja, odabrati bijeg u virtualnu stvarnost i 'egzistencijalnu liticu' ako ne razviju duhovni život i smisao.
Transhumanistički pogled, koji ljude svodi na kalkulatore, opasan je jer iskrivljuje razumijevanje ljudske prirode i može dovesti do smanjenja vrijednosti onih koji nisu 'najbolji kalkulatori'.
Crkva ima ključnu ulogu u suprotstavljanju transhumanizmu vraćanjem razumijevanja duše i ljudskosti, te korištenjem AI-ja kao alata za evangelizaciju i širenje istine.
AI nudi priliku za pristup učenju Crkve i duhovnom traženju na osoban i pristupačan način, posebno za one koji nisu tradicionalno povezani s Crkvom, ali imaju urođenu želju za Bogom.
Važno je djecu podučavati etičkom i odgovornom korištenju tehnologije, stvarajući prostore za slobodno vrijeme i iskustveno učenje, umjesto pasivnog konzumiranja sadržaja.
summary.title_en: What do we lose when we surrender our humanity to artificial intelligence | Catholic Futurist
summary.abstract_en: This podcast episode explores the deep challenges brought by artificial intelligence (AI) and its impact on humanity. Guests discuss the dangers of outsourcing human functions, transhumanism, and the potential 'existential cliff' due to spiritual poverty. The key role of the Church is emphasized in providing hope and guidance, and the possibilities of using AI are explored for evangelization and fostering human flourishing in the digital age.
key_points_en: Technology, especially AI, encourages the outsourcing of human functions and abilities, which leads to an epistemic shift in the way of knowing the world and distances us from embodied and social learning.
Excessive reliance on the ease and efficiency of technology avoids the reality that work is embedded in man and that through the struggle with reality, intellect and soul develop.
There is concern that people, with more free time due to AI, will choose escape into virtual reality and an 'existential cliff' if they do not develop spiritual life and meaning.

The transhumanist view, which reduces people to calculators, is dangerous because it distorts the understanding of human nature and can lead to a reduction in the value of those who are not 'the best calculators'.
The Church has a key role in opposing transhumanism by restoring the understanding of the soul and humanity, and by using AI as a tool for evangelization and spreading the truth.

AI offers an opportunity to access the teaching of the Church and spiritual seeking in a personal and accessible way, especially for those who are not traditionally connected with the Church, but have an innate desire for God.
It is important to teach children ethical and responsible use of technology, creating spaces for free time and experiential learning, instead of passive consumption of content.
